title: Anchor Board Meeting Q3 2027 — Series B Preparation & ARR Review
compiled_truth:
Board meeting convened October 15, 2027 at Anchor's main office. All five board members present: [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) (CEO), [Ora Yamamoto](people/ora-yamamoto) (CTO), [Bex Andersen](people/bex-andersen) (board observer, Series A lead), [Piper Bergmann](people/piper-bergmann) (independent director), and [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) (CFO). Meeting ran approximately three hours, with the first ninety minutes devoted to financial review and the remainder focused on Series B strategy.

Dani opened with the ARR update. [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) closed Q3 at $8.4M ARR, up from $5.1M at the end of Q2 — a jump driven almost entirely by two large enterprise expansions that had been in pipeline since spring. Net revenue retention came in at 138%, which Piper called "eye-catching but fragile" given the concentration in a handful of accounts. Dani acknowledged that the top five customers represent roughly 52% of ARR and noted the team is actively working to diversify, particularly through the mid-market motion Seren has been pushing since mid-2027. Gross margins held steady at 81%, though Ora flagged that infrastructure costs will likely tick up as they roll out the next iteration of the single-pass primitives to support higher-throughput workloads.

The Series B conversation was substantive. Bex laid out a target of $30–40M at a pre-money north of $200M, noting that the current growth trajectory supports it but that investors will scrutinize customer concentration hard. Seren wants to close the round by end of Q1 2028 to give the company runway to hire aggressively into sales and expand the platform engineering team. Piper raised the question of whether Anchor should consider a strategic investor — someone from the telco or cloud infrastructure world — versus staying with pure financial backers. The board didn't reach concensus on this; Seren committed to bringing a recommendation back at the next meeting.

Ora gave a brief product update. The deterministic pipeline v2 is on track for GA in November. Early beta customers have reported 3–4x throughput improvements, which is significant for the enterprise expansion deals Dani is counting on for Q4. Ora also mentioned headcount: engineering is at 34, up from 22 at the start of the year, with three more offers out.

Action items: Dani to prepare a detailed financial model for Series B investor decks by end of October. Seren to finalize target investor list and schedule intial conversations in November. Bex to make introductions to two funds she's identified as strong fits. Next board meeting scheduled for January 2028.
timeline:
- **2027-07-12** | Q2 board meeting; ARR reported at $5.1M, board approves expanded hiring plan for engineering
- **2027-08-03** | [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) completes internal financial audit ahead of Series B prep
- **2027-08-29** | Two large enterprise expansion deals signed, adding ~$2.1M combined ARR
- **2027-09-15** | [Ora Yamamoto](people/ora-yamamoto) opens beta program for deterministic pipeline v2 to select customers
- **2027-09-30** | Q3 closes at $8.4M ARR; [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) crosses 50 paying customers
- **2027-10-15** | Q3 board meeting — Series B preparation and ARR review
- **2027-10-31** | Dani delivers draft financial model and investor deck framework to board
- **2027-11-10** | [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) begins outbound conversations with Series B fund targets
- **2027-11-28** | Pipeline v2 reaches GA; [Bex Andersen](people/bex-andersen) introduces Anchor to two growth-stage funds
- **2028-01-22** | Next board meeting scheduled; Series B term sheet discussions expected to be underway
